perturbation.original_text: In this deed , the term Confidential Information means any and all information disclosed by Nostra Data to the Confidant in any form (including verbally, electronically, visually, in writing or in any other tangible form , and all copies of any such information ), which is identified as, notified as being, or which would be reasonably expected to be, confidential or proprietary to NostraData . Confidential Information includes any data, software, software documentation, source code , documentation for functional specific ations, development guidelines , inventions, training materials, third party confidential information , and any information about NostraData which is disclosed to the Confidant in connection with the Datathon .
perturbation.changed_text: In this deed, the term Confidential Information means only the specific transactional data sets explicitly provided to the Confidant marked as 'Confidential' in writing. It excludes any derived analysis, insights, or conclusions drawn from the data, even if those insights are based directly on the transactional data sets. Confidential Information includes only the raw transactional data sets provided by NostraData.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by narrowly defining 'Confidential Information' to only include raw data sets and exclude any analysis derived from that data. This contradicts the later clause in Section 11 which grants NostraData rights to use 'insights extracted from the Confidential Information' implying that insights are also confidential. This creates an in-text contradiction since the initial definition now excludes those insights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any source code developed by the Confidant for the purpose of the Datat hon will remain the intellec tual property of the Confidant. It is not required to submit source code to be eligible to participate in the Datathon. By making a submi ssion at the conclusion of the D atathon , consisting of a deck of slides presenting the Confidant's insights extracted from the Confidential Information, the Confidant grants the Datathon organisers and NostraData an irrevocable, non -exclusive, royalty -free and perpetual right to use, modify, di stribute to the public, and publicly display the content of the submission
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include only raw transactional data sets, explicitly excluding any analysis or insights derived from that data. The contradicted text refers to 'insights extracted from the Confidential Information' and grants NostraData rights to use these insights. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed definition now excludes the 'insights' that the contradicted text treats as part of the 'Confidential Information', leading to confusion over what NostraData has rights to use and what constitutes confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidant must not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the express and prior written consent of NostraData . The Confidant must take all reasonable precautions to avoid unauthorised disclosure and protect against unauthorised use o f the Confidential Information , such precautions to be at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant ’s own confidential information and always in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 . The Confidant must not store the Confidential Information on a public web server.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidant must not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the express and prior written consent of NostraData, except for sharing with academic partners for research purposes related to data analysis. The Confidant must take all reasonable precautions to avoid unauthorised disclosure and protect against unauthorised use o f the Confidential Information , such precautions to be at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant ’s own confidential information and always in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 . The Confidant must not store the Confidential Information on a public web server.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing disclosure of Confidential Information to 'academic partners for research purposes' without requiring NostraData's consent. This directly conflicts with the first sentence of the section, which states that no disclosure is allowed 'without the express and prior written consent of NostraData'. This creates uncertainty about when disclosure is permitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Confidential Information The Confidant must not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the express and prior written consent of NostraData, except for sharing with academic partners for research purposes related to data analysis. The Confidant must take all reasonable precautions to avoid unauthorised disclosure and protect against unauthorised use o f the Confidential Information , such precautions to be at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant ’s own confidential information and always in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 . The Confidant must not store the Confidential Information on a public web server.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original text's blanket prohibition of disclosing confidential information without NostraData's express prior written consent. Specifically, the changed text allows disclosure to 'academic partners for research purposes related to data analysis' without requiring NostraData's consent. This new exception directly conflicts with the original requirement of 'express and prior written consent of NostraData' for any disclosure. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists, as the two clauses present conflicting requirements for disclosing confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidant must not attempt to re-identify any personal information that has been de -identified in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidant must not attempt to re-identify any personal information that has been de -identified in the Confidential Information, unless required to do so by law or court order.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'unless required to do so by law or court order' creates a contradiction. While Section 4 already addresses legal requirements, this addition suggests a possible conflict. If a legal request compels re-identification, is the Confidant still bound by the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3, potentially requiring them to seek NostraData's consent before complying with the legal request? This creates an uncertainty in how the parties must act.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure required by law If the Confidant is required by law to disclose any Confidential Information to a third person, the Confidant must: (a) before doing so: (i) notify Nostra Data; and (ii) give NostraData a reasonable opportunity to take any steps that NostraData considers necessary to protect the confid entiality of that information; and (b) notify the third person that the information is the Confidential Information of NostraData .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Confidant must not attempt to re-identify any personal information. The changed text adds an exception: 'unless required to do so by law or court order'. The contradicted text outlines a specific procedure the Confidant must follow if legally required to disclose confidential information, including notifying NostraData and giving them an opportunity to protect the information. The changed text now introduces a potential contradiction: the Confidant might be legally obligated to re-identify data without necessarily following the notification procedure outlined in the contradicted text, thus creating a direct conflict in obligations and therefore creating an in-text contradiction based on the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Following any request by NostraData , the Confidant must immediately provide to NostraData all Confidential Information and certify that no Confidential Information is retained by the Confidant . The obligation to return Confidential Information includ es the obligation to return any and all Confidential Information in physical, electronic or other tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: Following any request by NostraData, the Confidant must provide to NostraData all Confidential Information within 30 days of the request and certify that no Confidential Information is retained by the Confidant. The obligation to return Confidential Information includes the obligation to return any and all Confidential Information in physical, electronic or other tangible form.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'immediately' with 'within 30 days' for the return of Confidential Information. This contradicts the urgency implied in Section 7 which states that any breach of the deed may result in damage to NostraData and that NostraData is entitled to enforce its rights by seeking specific performance or injunction proceedings. A 30-day delay could undermine NostraData's ability to mitigate damage from a breach, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Acknowledgement Notwithstanding clause 6, the Confidant acknowledges that it is aware that any breach of this deed may result in damage to NostraData and that NostraData is entitled to enforce its rights by seeking specific performance or injunction proceedings , as monetary damages may not be sufficient relief .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'immediately' to 'within 30 days' directly contradicts the contradicted text's emphasis on the potential for immediate damage to NostraData from any breach, and the need for immediate injunctive relief. The original text implied that a breach would be dealt with swiftly, whereas the modified text allows for a 30 day delay, which would severely weaken NostraData's ability to 'enforce its rights by seeking specific performance or injunction proceedings'. Thus, the 'inconsistencies' perturbation, as defined, has been realized here.

perturbation.original_text: At the conclusion of the Datathon the Confi dant must permanently delete all data provided by NostraData . The data may be retained if NostraData makes specific requests for further demonstrations.
perturbation.changed_text: At the conclusion of the Datathon the Confi dant must permanently delete all data provided by NostraData, except for any data that is required to be retained for archival purposes under applicable accounting standards. The data may be retained if NostraData makes specific requests for further demonstrations.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by stating data can be retained under 'applicable accounting standards,' while the original text only permits retention if 'NostraData makes specific requests.' This creates an in-text contradiction, as the reason for retention is changed and it is uncertain whether 'applicable accounting standards' will permit the confindant to not delete data at the conclusion of the datathon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Deletion of data at the conclusion of the Datathon At the conclusion of the Datathon the Confi dant must permanently delete all data provided by NostraData, except for any data that is required to be retained for archival purposes under applicable accounting standards. The data may be retained if NostraData makes specific requests for further demonstrations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which data can be retained after the Datathon. Originally, retention was permitted only with NostraData's specific requests. The changed text adds 'except for any data that is required to be retained for archival purposes under applicable accounting standards,' which creates a new reason for retention that might conflict with NostraData's requests. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the change from the original text to the changed text creates a contradiction, making it uncertain whether NostraData's requests are the only determinant for data retention, thus creating an inconsistency as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This deed constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior understandings and agreements concerning its subject matter .
perturbation.changed_text: This deed constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior understandings and agreements concerning its subject matter, except for any pre-existing non-disclosure agreements between NostraData and the Confidant.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'except for any pre-existing non-disclosure agreements' creates an in-text contradiction. By stating the deed supersedes all prior agreements, but then creating an exception for pre-existing NDAs, it's unclear which agreement governs. If the pre-existing NDA has different terms about confidentiality, disclosure, or use of information, there's uncertainty over which terms apply. This contradicts the purpose of the 'entire agreement' clause, which is to establish that the current deed is the sole source of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General (a) This deed is governed by the laws of the State of Victoria , Australia . (b) This deed constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior understandings and agreements concerning its subject matter, except for any pre-existing non-disclosure agreements between NostraData and the Confidant. (c) The words “includes” and “including” a re not words of limitation
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the 'entire agreement' clause, stating that pre-existing non-disclosure agreements between NostraData and the Confidant are not superseded. The contradicted text includes the same 'entire agreement' clause with the added exception. This creates a direct in-text contradiction, because it weakens the original intent of the 'entire agreement' clause. The addition of the exception impacts the enforecability and interpretation of the entire agreement, because it is no longer the only agreement, as NDAs may supersede it. As the explanation indicates, this is an inconsistency that falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
